headline: Transcript: Boeing races to avoid credit downgrade
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Claire Bushey and Taylor Nicole Rogers
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Boeing races to avoid credit downgrade’

Sonja HutsonGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, October 29th and this is your FT News Briefing. PwC’s revenues in Asia are sliding. And Boeing is desperately trying to avoid a junk credit rating. Plus, it’s getting harder to recruit election workers in the US.

Taylor Nicole RogersYou have this added element of there being a lot of threats against poll workers and even the election officials themselves.

Sonja HutsonI’m Sonja Hutson, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

PwC is losing market share in Asia. The firm reported yesterday that its revenue in the region dropped nearly 6 per cent in the fiscal year ending in June. You can blame the decline on two scandals one in China where regulators say PwC helped conceal fraud at the collapsed real estate developer Evergrande. The other scandal was in Australia.
